# Suite Format

Top-level YAML shape:

```yaml
suite_name: "name"
generated_at: "ISO timestamp"
goal_summary: "why this suite exists"
defaults:
  webhook_url: "http://localhost:3000/webhook/chatwoot"
  labels: ["ai"]
  assignee_name: "Bot"
  reply_timeout_seconds: 180
  reply_settle_seconds: 3
  poll_interval_seconds: 2
  inbox_id: 11
  captain_assistant_id: 80
cases:
  - case_id: "case-id"
    title: "human title"
    objective: "expected business outcome"
    opening_prompt: "optional opener used by adaptive mode"
    metadata:
      route_name: "optional routing metadata for the Codex planner"
      planned_user_turns:
        - "ordered Jira Test Data user intent/message for adaptive mode"
      jira_expected_result: "optional Zephyr expected result copied for traceability"
      stop_conditions:
        pass:
          - "Human-readable condition that means the case passed and should stop"
        fail:
          - "Human-readable condition that means the case failed and should stop"
    conversation_id: "optional existing conversation id"
    contact_id: "required with existing conversation_id"
    inbox_id: 11
    captain_assistant_id: 80
    labels: ["ai"]
    assignee_name: "Bot"
    contact:
      name: "Khánh Thị Thiện"
      phone: "0967142713"
      email: "optional"
    stop_regex_any:
      - "(?i)(mã vé|ticket code).*[A-Z0-9]{6,8}"
    fail_regex_any:
      - "(?i)(lỗi hệ thống|không thể xử lý)"
    steps:
      - prompt: "user turn"
        expectation:
          contains_any: ["fragment"]
          regex_any: ["pattern"]
```

Execution rules:

- if `conversation_id` is missing, runner creates a fresh contact and conversation
- if `conversation_id` is present, runner uses that conversation as-is
- step fails when reply is empty or does not satisfy the step expectation
- `stop_conditions.pass` / `stop_conditions.fail` are for QA review and UI editing
- `stop_regex_any` stops the case as pass; `fail_regex_any` stops the case as fail
- `scripts/interactive_chatwoot_loop.py` uses `opening_prompt` when present, otherwise it uses the first `steps[].prompt` as the opener and lets Codex CLI decide later turns from live bot replies
- suites generated by `scripts/build_suite_from_jira_cases.py --mode adaptive` put the first Jira `Test Data` line in `opening_prompt` and all parsed Jira turns in `metadata.planned_user_turns`; the adaptive planner treats those lines as ordered scenario intents and adapts wording/options to the real bot reply
